rationale: Build on the current best columnwise `mlp.fc.weight` int8 path instead of spending another run on projection-EMA scheduling. The new change keeps the winning transposed MLP expansion quantizer and projection EMA, but adds a tiny fp16 rescue for the worst per-row errors inside those transposed MLP matrices during both quant-aware roundtrip training and final serialization. That directly targets the final int8 zlib roundtrip metric while spending only a very small amount of the remaining size budget and keeping the script under the 1500-line limit.
title: Hybrid FP16 Rescue Rows For Transposed MLP Int8
